ALL_DISAPPEARS.description: No diagnosis condition of any severity is persisting.

APPEARS.description: The diagnosis condition indicated arises and/or persists.

DISAPPEARS.description: The diagnosis condition indicated does not longer persist. No diagnosis condition of the same severity is persisting for the affected channel.

DISAPPEARS_OTHER_REMAIN.description: The diagnosis condition indicated does not longer persist. Other diagnosis conditions of the same severity are persisting.

HI_LIM_EXCEEDED.description: Upper limit value exceeded. Only supported by RIOforPA channels.

LO_LIM_EXCEEDED.description: Lower limit value underrun. Only supported by RIOforPA channels.

SIMULATION_ACTIVE.description: Simulation is active. Only supported by RIOforPA channels.

MODE_CHANGED.description: Mode of block has changed. Only supported by RIOforPA channels.

SUBSTITUTE_VALUE_USED.description: Substitute value used. Only supported by RIOforPA channels.

Q_BAD_SUBSTITUTE_VALUE_USED.description: Process Image Qualifier = 0. Substitute value used by (Sub)Module. Only supported by RIOforFA channels.

ALL_DISAPPEARS.description: No diagnosis condition of any severity is persisting.

APPEARS.description: The diagnosis condition indicated arises and/or persists.

DISAPPEARS.description: The diagnosis condition indicated does not longer persist. No diagnosis condition of the same severity is persisting for the affected channel.

DISAPPEARS_OTHER_REMAIN.description: The diagnosis condition indicated does not longer persist. Other diagnosis conditions of the same severity are persisting.

HI_LIM_EXCEEDED.description: Upper limit value exceeded. Only supported by RIOforPA channels.

LO_LIM_EXCEEDED.description: Lower limit value underrun. Only supported by RIOforPA channels.

SIMULATION_ACTIVE.description: Simulation is active. Only supported by RIOforPA channels.

MODE_CHANGED.description: Mode of block has changed. Only supported by RIOforPA channels.

SUBSTITUTE_VALUE_USED.description: Substitute value used. Only supported by RIOforPA channels.

Q_BAD_SUBSTITUTE_VALUE_USED.description: Process Image Qualifier = 0. Substitute value used by (Sub)Module. Only supported by RIOforFA channels.

GOOD.description: For ConsumerStatus: The submodule’s IO data object could be successfully processed by the application process.  For ProviderStatus: The content of the submodule’s IO data object is valid.

BAD_BY_SUBSLOT.description: For ConsumerStatus: not used.  For ProviderStatus: The content of the submodule’s IO data object is invalid. The condition was detected by the submodule.

BAD_BY_SLOT.description: For ConsumerStatus: not used.  For ProviderStatus: . The content of the submodule’s IO data object is invalid. The condition was detected by the module.

BAD_BY_DEVICE.description: For ConsumerStatus: The IO device has locally detected problems to convey data.  For ProviderStatus: The content of the submodule’s IO data object is invalid. The condition was detected by the device.

BAD_BY_CONTROLLER.description: For ConsumerStatus: The submodule’s IO data object could not be successfully processed by the application of the Controller (e.g. because of the operation state “stop”).  For ProviderStatus: The content of the submodule’s IO data object is invalid. The condition was only locally detected by the Controller.

DIGITAL_0_24V.description: Digital, 0/24 V (discrete input or output only)

USE_SUBSTITUTE_VALUE.description: The value of the SubstituteValue configuration property is used as substitute value if an error condition is detected.

USE_LAST_VALID_VALUE.description: The last valid value is used as substitute value if an error condition is detected.

USE_ACTUAL_VALUE.description: No substitute value is used, the process value can have the wrong calculated value and status even if the error condition is detected.

Unspecified.description: No information about the status is given.

GOOD.description: For ConsumerStatus: The submodule’s IO data object could be successfully processed by the application process.  For ProviderStatus: The content of the submodule’s IO data object is valid.

BAD_BY_CONTROLLER.description: For ConsumerStatus: The submodule’s IO data object could not be successfully processed by the application of the Controller (e.g. because of the operation state “stop”).  For ProviderStatus: The content of the submodule’s IO data object is invalid. The condition was only locally detected by the Controller.

BAD_NOT_SPECIFIC.description: Proxy determines that a device does not communicate.

BAD_NOT_CONNECTED.description: Some communication error: the device is currently unavailable.

BAD_NOT_CONNECTED_SIMULATION_ACTIVE.description: Same as BAD_NOT_CONNECTED but with simulate flag set.

BAD_PASSIVATED_SIMULATION_ACTIVE.description: Same as BAD_PASSIVATED but with simulate flag set.

BAD_MAINTENANCE_ALARM.description: No measurement available because of a failure.

BAD_MAINTENANCE_ALARM_SIMULATION_ACTIVE.description: Same as BAD_MAINTENANCE_ALARM but with simulate flag set.

BAD_PROCESS.description: No measurement available because of invalid process conditions.

BAD_PROCESS_SIMULATION_ACTIVE.description: Same as BAD_PROCESS but with simulate flag set.

BAD_FUNCTION_CHECK_SIMULATION_ACTIVE.description: Same as BAD_FUNCTION_CHECK but with simulate flag set.

UNCERTAIN_SUBSTITUTE_SET.description: The configured substitute value (see SubstituteValue) is used as Process Value.

UNCERTAIN_SUBSTITUTE_SET_SIMULATION_ACTI.description: Same as UNCERTAIN_SUBSTITUTE_SET but with simulate flag set.

UNCERTAIN_INITIAL_VALUE.description: Default value since no measured value is available.

UNCERTAIN_INITIAL_VALUE_SIMULATION_ACTIV.description: Same as UNCERTAIN_INITIAL_VALUE but with simulate flag set.

UNCERTAIN_MAINTENANCE_DEMANDED_SIMULATIO.description: Same as UNCERTAIN_MAINTENANCE_DEMANDED but with simulate flag set.

UNCERTAIN_NO_MAINTENANCE.description: The process conditions are out of the specified operating range of the device.

UNCERTAIN_NO_MAINTENANCE_SIMULATION_ACTI.description: Same as UNCERTAIN_NO_MAINTENANCE but with simulate flag set.

GOOD.description: No error or special condition is associated with this value.

GOOD_SIMULATION_ACTIVE.description: Same as GOOD but with simulate flag set.

GOOD_INITIATE_FAULT_STATE.description: The value is from a block that wants its following output block (e.g. Actuator FB) to go to Fail Safe.

GOOD_MAINTENANCE_REQUIRED.description: Value is usable. Maintenance is recommended within a medium-term period.

GOOD_MAINTENANCE_REQUIRED_SIMULATION_ACT.description: Same as GOOD_MAINTENANCE_REQUIRED but with simulate flag set.

GOOD_MAINTENANCE_DEMANDED.description: Value is usable. Maintenance is strongly recommended within a short-term period.

GOOD_MAINTENANCE_DEMANDED_SIMULATION_ACT.description: Same as GOOD_MAINTENANCE_DEMANDED but with simulate flag set.

GOOD_LOCAL_OVERRIDE.description: The value is from a block that has been locked out.

GOOD_LOCAL_OVERRIDE_SIMULATION_ACTIVE.description: Same as GOOD_LOCAL_OVERRIDE_SIMULATED but with simulate flag set.

GOOD_FUNCTION_CHECK_SIMULATION_ACTIVE.description: Same as GOOD_FUNCTION_CHECK but with simulate flag set.

UNSPECIFIED.description: No information about the status is given.

GOOD.description: Input: The Process Value can be used by the Controller. Output: The Signal was generated from the Process Value.

UNCERTAIN.description: An error condition could compromise the Process Value (Input) or the Signal (Output).

BAD.description: Input: The Process Value cannot be used by the Controller. Output: The Signal may not be generated from the Process Value.

UNSPECIFIED.description: No information about the status is given.

DIGITAL_0_24V.description: Digital, 0/24 V (discrete input or output only)

FAILURE.description: Invalid signal due to malfunction of a sensor or actuator.

MAINTENANCE_REQUEST.description: Valid signal, but function could drop or cease soon.

OUT_OF_SPECIFICATION.description: Device is running beyond permissible range of some other parameter.

UNSPECIFIED.description: No information about the status is given.

USE_SUBSTITUTE_VALUE.description: The value of the SubstituteValue configuration property is used as substitute value if an error condition is detected.

USE_LAST_VALID_VALUE.description: The last valid value is used as substitute value if an error condition is detected.

USE_ACTUAL_VALUE.description: No substitute value is used, the process value can have the wrong calculated value and status even if the error condition is detected.

Unspecified.description: No information about the status is given.

ChassisComponent.description: Represents a chassis identifier based on the value of entPhysicalAlias object (defined in IETF RFC 2737) for a chassis component (i.e., an entPhysicalClass value of chassis(3))

InterfaceAlias.description: Represents a chassis identifier based on the value of ifAlias object (defined in IETF RFC 2863) for an interface on the containing chassis.

PortComponent.description: Represents a chassis identifier based on the value of entPhysicalAlias object (defined in IETF RFC 2737) for a port or backplane component (i.e., entPhysicalClass has a value of port(10), or backplane(4)), within the containing chassis.

MacAddress.description: Represents a chassis identifier based on the value of a unicast source address (encoded in network byte order and IEEE 802.3 canonical bit order) of a port on the containing chassis as defined in IEEE Std 802-2014.

NetworkAddress.description: Represents a chassis identifier based on a network address associated with a particular chassis. The encoded address is actually composed of two fields. The first field is a single octet, representing the IANA AddressFamilyNumbers value for the specific address type, and the second field is the network address value.

InterfaceName.description: Represents a chassis identifier based on the value of ifName object (defined in IETF RFC 2863) for an interface on the containing chassis.

Local.description: Represents a chassis identifier based on a locally defined value.

Insert.description: The target is added. An error occurs if a name conflict occurs.

Replace.description: Existing records are updated. An error occurs if a name cannot be matched to an existing record.

InsertOrReplace.description: Existing records are updated. New records are created if no match to an exising record.

Delete.description: Existing records are deleted. An error occurs if a name cannot be matched to an existing record.

Value.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

PublisherId.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

Value.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.
Stand-in for an unspecified data type.
Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

Unknown.description: Link is currently disconnected or initializing.

Value.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.
Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

OverrideValue.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

UserName.description: The rule specifies a UserName from a UserNameIdentityToken.

Thumbprint.description: The rule specifies the Thumbprint of a user or CA Certificate.

Role.description: The rule is a Role specified in an Access Token.

GroupId.description: The rule is a user group specified in the Access Token.

AuthenticatedUser.description: The rule specifies any non Anonymous UserIdentityToken.

Application.description: The rule specifies the combination of an application identity and an Anonymous UserIdentityToken.

X509Subject.description: The rule specifies the X509 subject name of a user or CA Certificate.

TrustedApplication.description: The rule specifies any trusted application that has been authenticated with a trusted ApplicationInstance Certificate.

Down.description: Not ready to pass packets and not in some test mode.
The interface does not pass any packets.

Testing.description: In some test mode. No operational packets can be passed.

Unknown.description: Status cannot be determined for some reason.

LowerLayerDown.description: Down due to state of lower-layer interface(s).

Value.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

Other.description: System has capabilities other than those listed below

WlanAccessPoint.description: System has WLAN access point capability

DocsisCableDevice.description: System has DOCSIS cable device capability (IETF RFC 4639)

TwoPortMacRelay.description: System has Two-port MAC Relay (TPMR) functionality.

Value.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.
Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

PortRef.description: Interface based on the port-ref MIB object.

SystemPortNumber.description: Interface based on the system port number.

Value.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

InProgress.description: The auto-negotiation protocol is running and negotiation is currently in-progress.

Complete.description: The auto-negotiation protocol has completed successfully.

Unknown.description: The auto-negotiation status is not currently known, this could be because it is still negotiating or the protocol cannot run (e.g., if no medium is present).

NoNegotiation.description: No auto-negotiation is executed. The auto-negotiation function is either not supported on this interface or has not been enabled.

SupportInitialPasswordChange.description: Indicates if the server supports the feature to require a password change after the creation of the user.

SupportDisableUser.description: Indicates if the server supports to disable a user.

SupportDisableDeleteForUser.description: Indicates if the server supports the configuration NoDelete for a user.

SupportNoChangeForUser.description: Indicates if the server supports the configuration NoChangeByUser for a user.

SupportDescriptionForUser.description: Indicates if the server supports to management of a description for the user.

RequiresUpperCaseCharacters.description: Indicates if a upper case ASCII character is required in a password.

RequiresLowerCaseCharacters.description: Indicates if a lower case ASCII character is required in a password.

RequiresDigitCharacters.description: Indicates if a digit ASCII character is required in a password.

RequiresSpecialCharacters.description: Indicates if a special character is required in a password.

InterfaceAlias.description: Represents a port identifier based on the ifAlias MIB object defined in IETF RFC 2863.

PortComponent.description: Represents a port identifier based on the value of entPhysicalAlias (defined in IETF RFC 2737) for a port component (i.e., entPhysicalClass value of port(10) or backplane(4)), within the containing chassis.

MacAddress.description: Represents a port identifier based on a unicast source address (encoded in network byte order and IEEE 802.3 canonical bit order) which has been detected by the agent and associated with a particular port (IEEE Std 802-2014).

NetworkAddress.description: Represents a port identifier based on a network address, detected by the agent and associated with a particular port.

InterfaceName.description: Represents a port identifier based on the ifName MIB object, defined in IETF RFC 2863.

AgentCircuitId.description: Represents a port identifier based on the agent-local identifier of the circuit (defined in IETF RFC 3046), detected by the agent and associated with a particular port.

Local.description: Represents a port identifier based on a value locally assigned.

SuppressCertificateExpired.description: Ignore errors related to the validity time of the Certificate.

SuppressHostNameInvalid.description: Ignore mismatches between the host name or ApplicationUri.

SuppressRevocationStatusUnknown.description: Ignore errors if the revocation list cannot be found for the issuer of the Certificate.

SuppressIssuerCertificateExpired.description: Ignore errors if an issuer has an expired Certificate.

SuppressIssuerRevocationStatusUnknown.description: Ignore errors if the revocation list cannot be found for any issuer of issuer Certificates.

StreamIdInUse.description: StreamID in use by another Talker

StreamDestinationAddressInUse.description: Stream destination address already in use

PriorityIsNotAnSrcClass.description: Requested priority is not an SR Class priority

MaxFrameSizeTooLarge.description: MaxFrameSize is too large for media

FirstValueChangedForStreamId.description: Changes in FirstValue for a registered StreamID

VlanBlockedOnEgress.description: VLAN is blocked on this egress port (Registration Forbidden)

VlanTaggingDisabledOnEgress.description: VLAN tagging is disabled on this egress port (untagged set)

FeatureNotPropagated.description: Enhanced feature cannot be propagated to original Port

BridgeDoesNotProvideNetworkId.description: Nearest Bridge cannot provide network identification for stream transformation

StreamIdTypeNotSupported.description: Stream identification type not supported for stream transformation

FeatureNotSupported.description: Enhanced feature cannot be supported without a CNC

PartialFailed.description: One or more Listeners ready, and one or more Listeners failed.

Disabled.description: The related TSN Stream is currently disabled.

Configuring.description: The related TSN Stream is in the process of receiving configuration parameters from the TSN Control Layer.

Ready.description: The related TSN Stream has successfully received and applied the configuration from the TSN Control Layer. The related TSN Stream is not fully operational as long as local preconditions (e.g. synchronization state) are not valid.

Operational.description: The related TSN Stream object is configured and all other required preconditions (e.g. synchronization state) for sending / receiving data are valid.

Error.description: The related TSN Stream object is in an error state.

Value.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

Body.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

NoChangeByUser.description: The user cannot change the password.

MustChangePassword.description: The user must change the password to get the assigned roles. If the password is not changed, the user has only the Role Anonymous.

Value.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.
Stand-in for an unspecified data type.
Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

FAILURE.description: Malfunction of the device or any of its peripherals.

OFF_SPEC.description: The device is currently working outside of its specified range or that internal diagnoses indicate deviations from measured or set values.

MAINTENANCE_REQUIRED.description: This element is working, but a maintenance operation is required.

Visual.description: Location indication through optical signals, such as blinking LEDs, if supported by the device.

Audible.description: Location indication through auditory signals, such as sounds or beeps, if supported by the device.

Firmware.description: The software update is used for the firmware of a physical device.

Application.description: The software update is used for an executable software.

Configuration.description: The software update is used for the configuration of a device or application.

Solution.description: The software update is used to install a solution with several software package to several components.

Current.description: The currently used version of the software identified by the CurrentVersion Object.

Pending.description: The pending version of the software that could be installed identified by the PendingVersion Object.

Fallback.description: The fallback version of the software identified by the FallbackVersion Object.

KeepsParameters.description: If KeepsParameters is not set, the device will lose its configuration during update. The Client should do a backup of the parameters before the update and restore them afterwards.

WillDisconnect.description: If WillDisconnect is set, the OPC UA Server will restart during installation. This can be the case if the update is about the firmware of the device that hosts the OPC UA Server.

RequiresPowerCycle.description: If RequiresPowerCycle is set, the devices require a manual power off / power on for installation.

WillReboot.description: If WillReboot is set, the device will reboot during the update, inclusive of embedded infrastructure elements like an integrated switch. An update Client should take this into account since the devices behind an integrated switch are not reachable for that time.

NeedsPreparation.description: If NeedsPreparation is not set, the Client can install the update without maintaining the PrepareForUpdateStateMachine. This can be used to support an installation without stopping the software.
